The '''Great Goblin''' was a mute, [[Anx/Legends|Anx]] [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] who fought in the gladiatorial pits of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Loovria/Legends|Loovria]]. He died of terror after being purchased by a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Unidentified Hutt crime lord (Great Goblin)|crime lord]] who tried to transport him alone to the planet [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]].

==Biography==
The mute, [[Anx/Legends|Anx]] [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] known as the Great Goblin fought in the gladiatorial pits of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Loovria/Legends|Loovria]], where [[Gender/Legends|he]] set a record for the most [[Death/Legends|kills]] that would remain unbroken for at least three hundred [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. The Anx was [[Slavery/Legends|sold]] to a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Unidentified Hutt crime lord (Great Goblin)|crime lord]], who had him transported to the planet [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] in a small [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]. The gladiator was transported alone and died of terror on the second [[Standard day/Legends|day]] of the trip, as members of the Anx species had a need for company. The gladiator and the Hutt were later written about by the [[sentientologist]] [[Tem Eliss]] in The [[University of Sanbra]] [[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life|Guide to Intelligent Life]].<ref name="SWG7" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Great Goblin was first mentioned in ''[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Anx]]'', an article written by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]] for the [[Star Wars Gamer 7|seventh]] issue of [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' magazine ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' in [[2001]]. The gladiator was later mentioned in the [[2014]] [[StarWars.com]] article ''[[Galactic Gladiators: Great Warriors of Star Wars]]'' by [[Tim Veekhoven]] and [[Kevin Beentjes]]. It is not stated what era the Goblin lived in, although due to a mention of the [[Battle of Gravlex Med]], the in-universe article from ''Star Wars Gamer'' must be written in the [[New Republic era]] or later.
